what_it_does: A standalone @dsCard preview (group 'Brand') demonstrating small status indicators: filled status dots, soft-fill calendar task chips, status/dropdown pills, and a notification bell with a red count badge plus an avatar.
observations: Comment header `@dsCard group="Brand" name="Status dots, chips, bell" subtitle="Dots · task chips · pills · notification badge" viewport="700x260"`.
Four labelled rows: Status dots, Task chips, Pills/dropdowns, Bell + avatar.
Status dots reuse the status colours (#5A8A4A, #E5C547, #E5A547, #C24A3C, #4A78B8, #A89678).
Task chips pair a soft-fill bg with the matching dot and a label (Site survey, Floorplan v2, Reshoot lobby, Hub copy) — examples drawn from a property/real-estate-ish domain.
Pills include a status pill, a 'Task Type ▾' dropdown, and a dark 'MENU ▴' pill using --bg-dark/--fg-inverse.
Bell is an inline SVG with a red circular badge (#F04325) showing '7'; lead notes the badge has no '+'.
Avatar is a 28px circle on --bg-muted with a placeholder glyph.
standouts: Doctrine line: status uses filled dots; task chips inherit the dot colour at a soft-fill bg; the bell badge is a small red circle with no '+'.
The red badge colour #F04325 is hardcoded and distinct from the status-error #C24A3C.
conventions: Uses tokens for chrome (--bg-canvas, --r-sm/xs, --bg-dark, --fg-inverse) but hardcodes status/badge hexes inline.
Reuses the same status hex palette as colors_status.html (consistent across cards) but as literals, not vars.
concerns: Status/badge colours are hardcoded literals here rather than referenced as tokens, so they could drift from the status colour card if either changes.
notes: A components card showing how status colour translates into dots, task chips, pills, and a no-plus notification badge.
questions: Why is the notification badge #F04325 rather than the defined error token #C24A3C — is the badge a separate intentional colour?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — metadata promises dots/chips/pills/badge and the body demonstrates each with usage doctrine.
apparent_themes: Status colour applied consistently across small UI indicators
Soft-fill chip pattern inheriting a status dot colour
Minimal notification badge convention (red circle, no plus)
intention_signals: Aims to show the applied form of the status palette — how the abstract colours become dots, chips, pills, and badges — and to pin the badge convention.
novelty: The explicit no-'+' badge rule and the dot-colour-inheriting task-chip pattern.
contribution: Contributes the applied status-indicator vocabulary (dots, soft-fill task chips, pills, notification badge, avatar) that a fused system would keep as its small-indicator/badge reference.
summary_for_embedding: A Brand-group design-system preview card demonstrating status dots, soft-fill task chips, status/dropdown pills, a red no-plus notification bell badge, and an avatar.
intention_for_embedding: To show how the status colour system is applied across small UI indicators and to fix the notification-badge convention.
